deployment_question: How does the model perform in the exact population and setting where it will be used?
definition: Construct validation data to match the intended deployment population, setting, measurement process, and decision context. This is a targeted operating point rather than a generic hardest split.

split_operator.spectra_mapping: selects the curve point corresponding to the intended-use distribution

implementation_recipe: Write the intended-use population, setting, and prediction time before splitting.
Select validation samples that satisfy that target definition.
Report whether training data did or did not include the target setting.
quality_gates: Pre-specify the target setting and eligibility rule.
Report calibration, clinical utility, and subgroup performance inside the target setting.
Distinguish target validation from broad external generalizability.
failure_modes: A validation set can be external but irrelevant to the intended use.
Target setting data can be too small for stable estimates.
Eligibility changes after deployment can move the operating point.

citations.title: Targeted validation: validating clinical prediction models in their intended population and setting
citations.evidence: Defines targeted validation as validation in the intended population and setting.

citations.title: Perspectives on validation of clinical predictive algorithms
citations.evidence: Distinguishes temporal, geographical, and domain generalizability for clinical algorithms.
